brief_justification: Correctly identifies Yes with precise reasoning about relative vs. absolute metrics; Multi-level analysis with scenario table, metric-by-metric audit, and logical error identification; No explicit self-monitoring or named bias checks — reasoning is presented linearly without reflection on method; Scenario table functions as boundary-condition verification but no re-derivation from a different angle; Distinguishes what ELL data does show vs. what aggregate/relative metrics cannot prove, without structured confidence levels; Explicitly enumerates 4 alternative scenarios and explains why only B supports the claim while C/D remain viable; Every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation — table and metric walkthrough are fully reproducible; Avoids the uniform-progress-means-on-track anti-pattern in substantive reasoning but does not explicitly name or address the monitoring-loop negative gate; Explicitly flags correlation-to-causation suppression and avoids forward momentum bias; response is linear without branching, looping, or gate structures despite topology prescribing a convergence loop with EMA re-weighting and M-node reflection.
